allies.rationale_text: Second-order-thinking strengthens regulatory-horizon-scanning by pushing the team to visualize downstream dominoes before rules fully arrive.
Systems-thinking strengthens regulatory-horizon-scanning by converting static regulation snapshots into changing interdependent system patterns.
allies.source_quote: seeing interrelationships and patterns of change rather than static snapshots

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias weakens regulatory-horizon-scanning when teams collect policy signals mainly to support the roadmap they already prefer.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Regulatory-horizon-scanning conflicts with status-quo-bias when organizations defer compliance adaptation until forced by enforcement.
structured_tensions.source_quote: regulatory-horizon-scanning vs status-quo-bias: organizations defer compliance adaptation until forced by enforcement.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Regulatory-horizon-scanning conflicts with commitment-bias when sunk roadmap choices block timely policy pivot.
structured_tensions.source_quote: regulatory-horizon-scanning vs commitment-bias: sunk roadmap choices block timely policy pivot.

curation_notes.summary: Relation surface stays inside the current graph vocabulary and keeps omission stronger than weak forcing.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 3 target ids or import donor-shaped relation neighborhoods to rescue weak local grounding.
open_questions: Some source-real neighbors remain omitted because the current graph vocabulary would force weaker mappings than this conservative pass allows.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only and is noted only in batch artifacts.
antagonists: No multihop or higher-order semantics were introduced in these Wave 3 files.
structured_tensions: Explicit tensions were preserved only where the raw source clearly earned a current-vocabulary target.
